In any case, Trump=E2=80=99s desire to be surrounded by yes-men and yes-wom=
en=E2=80=94Lewandowski literally wrote a book called Let Trump Be Trump=E2=
=80=94feels like an unnecessary distraction to several on the campaign. =E2=
=80=9CThe only thing that Donald Trump needs to do is stop talking about [K=
amala=E2=80=99s] race and gender and focus on
the policies,=E2=80=9D said one Trump advisor. =E2=80=9CWhat is Corey going=
 to do, make up some magic bullet? Everyone knows what has to be done; Trum=
p just has to do it.=E2=80=9D

Easier said than done, of course. After Trump=E2=80=99s disastrous appearan=
ce at the National Association of Black Journalists, where he accused Harri=
s of deciding to =E2=80=9Cbecome Black,=E2=80=9D a campaign source told me =
that there was an all-hands call to encourage staffers to ignore the =E2=80=
=9Coutside noise,=E2=80=9D which some interpreted as a reference to rumors =
of a leadership change. =E2=80=9CThey=E2=80=99re all very stressed,=E2=80=
=9D said the campaign source. =E2=80=9CVery, very stressed. They=E2=80=99re=
 finally coming to grips that change is coming.=E2=80=9D

Trump, after all, has a habit of summer house cleanings. In June 2016, he o=
usted Lewandowski for Conway, and in July 2020, he fired Brad Parscale. Ano=
ther shake-up appears to be underway. I=E2=80=99m told that Trump is being =
goaded by his outside kitchen cabinet and former advisors to take
action, with these provocateurs criticizing the failure of the Wiles-LaCivi=
ta campaign to define Harris immediately after Biden=E2=80=99s exit. There=
=E2=80=99s also plenty of finger-pointing over Trump=E2=80=99s light schedu=
le. As usual, there=E2=80=99s no shortage of people in Trump=E2=80=99s ear,=
 assuring him that someone else is to blame.

          <td style=3D"padding-bottom: 36px; color: #000000; font-size: 16p=
x; line-height: 30px; margin-top: 0; margin-bottom: 0;">So much for <b>Dona=
ld Trump</b>=E2=80=99s tightly run, professionally operated, supposedly imp=
enetrable campaign. Last weekend, I began hearing rumblings that the Red Bu=
ll-chugging operative <b>Corey Lewandowski</b>, who managed and then was fi=
red from Trump=E2=80=99s 2016 campaign=E2=80=94and then was fired <i>again<=
/i> from the super PAC in 2021 after being accused of sexually assaulting a=
 donor=E2=80=94was calling around and boasting that he would be returning a=
s campaign chairman, a role that would place him <i>above </i>campaign co-m=
anagers <b>Susie Wiles</b> and <b>Chris LaCivita</b>. Lewandowski, of cours=
e, is something of a scandal magnet, with a Wikipedia that doubles as a ver=
itable rap sheet. But Trump, under siege and self-sabotaging as <b>Kamala H=
arris </b>surges in the polls, has been feeling superstitious and nostalgic=
 of late. <br/><br/>I woke up Thursday
morning to a storm of text messages saying that it was <i>really happening<=
/i>, and then, within an hour, Trump=E2=80=99s team had leaked the news to =
Politico. The two had been talking for a while, and Lewandowski traveled wi=
th the team on the night of the debate. But from what I hear, Trump was alo=
ne in making the call to hire Lewandowski, who has been consulting for the =
R.N.C. since April. =E2=80=9CPeople in Trumpworld try to stop things and th=
ey can=E2=80=99t,=E2=80=9D said a former aide. =E2=80=9CSometimes when the =
ship has left the port, it=E2=80=99s left the port.=E2=80=9D<br/> <br/>Shee=
pishly, perhaps, the news of Lewandowski=E2=80=99s reinstatement was bundle=
d with a handful of other, lesser-known new hires: <b>Taylor Budowich</b>, =
<b>Alex Pfeiffer</b>, <b>Alex Bruesewitz</b>, and <b>Tim Murtaugh</b>=E2=80=
=94all =E2=80=9Cveterans of prior Trump campaigns=E2=80=9D with =E2=80=9Cun=
matched experience,=E2=80=9D per a campaign statement. Spokesperson <b>Stev=
en Cheung</b> told me Lewandowski=E2=80=99s title will be =E2=80=9Csenior a=
dvisor,=E2=80=9D and that Wiles and
LaCivita will remain as co-campaign managers. (Trump himself referred to Le=
wandowski during a press conference on Thursday afternoon as a =E2=80=9Cper=
sonal envoy or something.=E2=80=9D) <br/><br/>All around Bedminster, where =
Trump has relocated to escape the South Florida heat, there is a pervasive =
anxiety that the candidate is trying to recreate the chaos that surrounded =
his winning 2016 campaign. No one thinks Lewandowski and LaCivita can cohab=
itate for long, leading some people close to Trump to speculate that he=E2=
=80=99s trying to push LaCivita out, just as he installed <b>Anthony Scaram=
ucci</b> to fire <b>Steve Bannon</b> and <b>Reince Priebus</b>. =E2=80=9CSu=
sie is a survivor; she=E2=80=99s not going anywhere. But then you have LaCi=
vita and Corey Lewandowski, two alpha men,=E2=80=9D said a source close to =
Lewandowski. =E2=80=9CIt=E2=80=99s like Trump just wants them to kill each =
other and for one to win so he doesn=E2=80=99t have to actually fire anyone=
=2E=E2=80=9D <br/><br/>One obvious vulnerability facing LaCivita is his
astronomical fee. As Trump stews over his fading poll numbers and whether a=
 once easily winnable election is slipping away, there has been growing cha=
tter in some corners of Mar-a-Lago about the $50,000 that LaCivita=E2=80=99=
s firm, Advanced Strategies, collects from the campaign and R.N.C. each mon=
th, which is included in the nearly $1.7 million he=E2=80=99s invoiced the =
campaign so far this year for various services like placed media, political=
 strategy consulting, and video production, up from the $1.65 million he bi=
lled last year. (Sure, it=E2=80=99s not <b>Jeff Roe</b> money, but it has s=
ome tongues wagging.) =E2=80=9CI have never told anyone I will be conductin=
g a forensic audit of the campaign, nor have I alluded to, or have any unde=
rstanding of, how much money Chris LaCivita may or may not have billed this=
 campaign,=E2=80=9D Lewandowski told me. <br/><br/>Cheung, the Trump campai=
gn spokesman, dismissed the notion that Lewandowski has been hired to drive=
 out LaCivita as =E2=80=9Cnot true=E2=80=9D and =E2=80=9Cfake
news.=E2=80=9D Nevertheless, the typically jovial LaCivita is now quiet and=
 moody, I hear, a dramatic change from the heady days of the Republican Nat=
ional Convention, last month, when he was boasting about the size of his ho=
tel suite. <br/></td>

          <td style=3D"padding-bottom: 36px; color: #000000; font-size: 16p=
x; line-height: 30px; margin-top: 0; margin-bottom: 0;">Around 11 a.m. this=
 morning, shortly after the Lewandowski announcement, Trump=E2=80=99s polls=
ter <b>Tony</b> <b>Fabrizio</b>, along with LaCivita and Wiles, hosted a =
=E2=80=9Cstakeholders call,=E2=80=9D essentially giving campaign updates to=
 many of Trump=E2=80=99s outside confidants. The invite list was a sort of =
who=E2=80=99s who of Trumpworld past: Priebus, <b>Kellyanne</b> <b>Conway</=
b>, <b>Roger</b> <b>Stone</b>, <b>David</b> <b>Bossie</b>, <b>Mike</b> <b>L=
indell</b>, <b>Brooke</b> <b>Rollins</b>, <b>Andy Surabian</b>, <b>Bill Ste=
pien</b>, <b>Eric Trump</b>, <b>David Urban</b>, and <b>Ric</b> <b>Grenell<=
/b>, among others. It was seen as a way to keep them looped in, united, and=
 feeling positive about the campaign=E2=80=99s prospects. <br/><br/>But the=
re=E2=80=99s no question the Trump operation has been on tilt since the <b>=
Biden</b>-Harris switcheroo, and will likely become even
<i>more</i> unbalanced as larger-than-life figures like Lewandowski return =
to the arena. I=E2=80=99m hearing Lewandowski has told allies that he=E2=80=
=99s been mandated by Trump to build his own all-star roster of alumni from=
 the 2016 campaign, which could eventually rival the tight-knit LaCivita an=
d Wiles loyalists currently in place. =E2=80=9CThere are things and people =
that swirl around Lewandowski, and if you bring him back on, they=E2=80=99r=
e coming with him,=E2=80=9D said the source close to Lewandowski. =E2=80=9C=
He=E2=80=99s a mini tornado. He stirs things up and brings things into the =
orbit.=E2=80=9D<br/><br/>As I <u><a href=3D"https://email.puck.news/e/c/eyJ=
lbWFpbF9pZCI6ImRnVDJ4Z1lEQUt2UktxclJLZ0dSV01DalFNeHBBLVQ4bEtLS1piND0iLCJocm=
VmIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9wdWNrLm5ld3MvY2FuLWtlbGx5YW5uZS1jb253YXktZ2V0LXRydW1wLXRoZ=
S1hdHRlbnRpb24taGUtY3JhdmVzLz91dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249VGhlK0Jlc3QrJTI2K1RoZStCcmln=
aHRlc3QrLStTVUJTQ1JJQkVSUyslMjg4JTJGMTUlMkYyNCUyOVx1MDAyNnV0bV9jb250ZW50PVR=
oZStCZXN0KyUyNitUaGUrQnJpZ2h0ZXN0Ky0rU1VCU0NSSUJFUlMrJTI4NyUyRjI0JTJGMjQlMj=
lcdTAwMjZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsX2FjdGlvblx1MDAyNnV0bV9zb3VyY2U9Y3VzdG9tZXIua=
W9cdTAwMjZ1dG1fdGVybT1mNmM2MDYxN2RhNDJhYmQxMmEiLCJpbnRlcm5hbCI6ImY2YzYwNjE3=
ZGE0MmFiZDEyYSIsImxpbmtfaWQiOjk4ODczM30/9f5b0e7af01f203d795ac75477f3e76e39f=
8bea2b095aaaa15391c621b7d07de" style=3D"color:#d40000;text-decoration:none;=
font-weight:bold" class=3D"sdf">reported</a></u> last week, Trump has also =
spent considerable time conferring with his former campaign manager and sen=
ior
advisor Kellyanne Conway. Sources tell me that she=E2=80=99s still on the f=
ence about joining the campaign, especially given her contract as a Fox New=
s contributor. But Conway is already billing the R.N.C. around $30,000 per =
month, and switching over to the Trump campaign payroll would be little mor=
e than a formality. And as for potential turf wars, Conway seems to believe=
 there=E2=80=99s plenty of campaign to go around. =E2=80=9CThere=E2=80=99s =
no more silos, no more turf battles, the field is long and wide enough for =
the existing campaign structure and personnel that have brought [Trump] thi=
s far to be supplemented=E2=80=94but not substituted=E2=80=94by new players=
,=E2=80=9D she told me. <br/><br/>In any case, Trump=E2=80=99s desire to be=
 surrounded by yes-men and yes-women=E2=80=94Lewandowski literally wrote a =
book called <i>Let Trump Be Trump</i>=E2=80=94feels like an unnecessary dis=
traction to several on the campaign. =E2=80=9CThe only thing that Donald Tr=
ump needs to do is stop talking about [Kamala=E2=80=99s] race and gender an=
d focus on
the policies,=E2=80=9D said one Trump advisor. =E2=80=9CWhat is Corey going=
 to do, make up some magic bullet? Everyone knows what has to be done; Trum=
p just has to do it.=E2=80=9D  <br/><br/>Easier said than done, of course. =
After Trump=E2=80=99s disastrous appearance at the National Association of =
Black Journalists, where he accused Harris of deciding to =E2=80=9Cbecome B=
lack,=E2=80=9D a campaign source told me that there was an all-hands call t=
o encourage staffers to ignore the =E2=80=9Coutside noise,=E2=80=9D which s=
ome interpreted as a reference to rumors of a leadership change. =E2=80=9CT=
hey=E2=80=99re all very stressed,=E2=80=9D said the campaign source. =E2=80=
=9CVery, very stressed. They=E2=80=99re finally coming to grips that change=
 is coming.=E2=80=9D <br/><br/>Trump, after all, has a habit of summer hous=
e cleanings. In June 2016, he ousted Lewandowski for Conway, and in July 20=
20, he fired <b>Brad Parscale</b>. Another shake-up appears to be underway.=
 I=E2=80=99m told that Trump is being goaded by his outside kitchen cabinet=
 and former advisors to take
action, with these provocateurs criticizing the failure of the Wiles-LaCivi=
ta campaign to define Harris <i>immediately </i>after Biden=E2=80=99s exit.=
 There=E2=80=99s also plenty of finger-pointing over Trump=E2=80=99s light =
schedule. As usual, there=E2=80=99s no shortage of people in Trump=E2=80=99=
s ear, assuring him that someone else is to blame. <br/></td>
